_meta.purpose: Toxicology / therapeutic drug monitoring (TDM) reference ranges. INTERPRETATION domain (no image model): the rules engine + LLM read a measured drug/toxin level and this table to flag therapeutic vs toxic. Units are stated per analyte. Decision support only; clinical toxicology + poisons centre guide management.
_meta.usage: For a result value V of key K: subtherapeutic if V<therapeutic_low; therapeutic if in [low,high]; toxic if >=toxic; critical if >=critical or a critical_flag applies. Levels are drawn at the stated timing.
_meta.source: Standard TDM / clinical toxicology references (Micromedex; TIETZ; local poisons guidance). Curated for JORINOVA NEXUS — verify against local assay units.

paracetamol.name: Paracetamol (acetaminophen)
paracetamol.note: Plot 4 h level on nomogram; start N-acetylcysteine if above treatment line or staggered/unknown time.

salicylate.name: Salicylate (aspirin)
salicylate.note: >500 moderate, >700 severe -> urinary alkalinisation / haemodialysis. Watch mixed acid-base.

digoxin.name: Digoxin
digoxin.note: Toxicity worse with hypokalaemia/renal failure; consider DigiFab. Correlate with K+.

lithium.name: Lithium
lithium.note: >1.5 toxic; >2.5 or symptomatic -> haemodialysis. Check renal function/hydration.

phenytoin.name: Phenytoin
phenytoin.note: Correct for albumin; free phenytoin better if hypoalbuminaemia/renal failure.

valproate.name: Valproic acid
valproate.note: Hyperammonaemia can occur even at therapeutic levels.

vancomycin.name: Vancomycin (trough)
vancomycin.note: Target AUC/MIC; troughs >20 raise nephrotoxicity risk.

gentamicin.name: Gentamicin (trough)
gentamicin.note: High trough -> nephro/ototoxicity; use extended-interval dosing nomogram for peak.

ethanol.name: Ethanol
ethanol.note: >80 mg/dL legal-impairment range; >400 potentially fatal. Check osmolar gap for co-ingestants.

methanol.name: Methanol
methanol.note: CRITICAL: raised anion-gap metabolic acidosis + osmolar gap -> fomepizole/ethanol + haemodialysis.

ethylene_glycol.name: Ethylene glycol
ethylene_glycol.note: CRITICAL: anion-gap acidosis + oxalate crystalluria -> fomepizole + haemodialysis.

carboxyhemoglobin.name: Carboxyhaemoglobin (CO)
carboxyhemoglobin.note: Non-smoker <3%, smoker <10%. >25% or neuro/cardiac -> consider hyperbaric O2.

methemoglobin.name: Methaemoglobin
methemoglobin.note: >15% symptomatic; >30% -> methylene blue (avoid in G6PD deficiency).
